mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated with month-over-month decreases in FEDFUNDS over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income expectations, reducing corporate revenue forecasts and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates broad-based final demand expansion that outpaces supply, leading to positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and INDPRO do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth signals economic expansion, and in the post-recession recovery regime labor supply constraints remain slack, keeping wage pressures muted and allowing corporate margins to expand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 during the 3 years following an NBER recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, month-over-month declines in housing starts signal that restrictive monetary policy has suppressed interest-rate-sensitive demand, confirming a slowing macro environment that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in HOUST when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects improving manufacturing utilization that translates directly into operating leverage and earnings beats, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in real retail sales signal weakening consumer demand that reduces nominal revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment indicate a self-reinforcing demand expansion where construction employment feeds into broader consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly rising HOUST and falling UNRATE fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
